heading: Ashenfang Longsword
first_paragraph: A balanced battlefield blade with a smoldering fuller and leather-wrapped grip suited to mounted charges and evening duels.Forged for Emberfall wardens who guarded the high bridges at the edge of the ash plains.

heading: Bastion Pike Chestplate
first_paragraph: A practical heavy chestplate with reinforced ribs and broad pauldron hooks built for long marches and shieldwall duty.Stamped with the old pike sigil of border militias that held the line through long autumn rains.

heading: Dreadmarsh Graveguard Harness
first_paragraph: A dark harness of layered plates and chain that exhales a hint of cold bog air when struck.Recovered from a burial mire where pale reeds swayed over still black water.

heading: Garrison Oathblade
first_paragraph: A straightforward service blade with excellent balance, easy upkeep, and a broad guard suited to daily patrol work.Issued to city watch captains in the western marches and polished at the close of every shift.

heading: Kingshade Bastard Sword
first_paragraph: A hand-and-a-half sword with a blackened blade and silver runes, prized for its reach, weight, and graceful finishing cuts.Rumored to have hung above the throne of a vanished king in the north.

heading: Lionwake Tournament Cuirass
first_paragraph: A gleaming tournament cuirass with elegant fluting and hidden buckles that remains dependable in live combat.Worn by lancers in summer tourneys where banners snapped above the lists.

heading: Mistglass Infiltrator Wrap
first_paragraph: A translucent wrap-cloak with a strange sheen, ideal for rooftop routes and quick disappearances.Cut to disappear against city rain and lamp haze.

heading: Moonlit Tournament Blade
first_paragraph: A polished blade that moves easily from the tournament lists to moonlit monster contracts.Commonly carried by hedge champions on bright riverland tournament days.

heading: Stonewarden Bulwark Plate
first_paragraph: A dense chestplate etched with defensive sigils, built to turn crushing blows into manageable impacts.Blessed in mountain shrines before every siege season beneath prayer flags and cedar smoke.

heading: Sunset Falcon Travel Cloak
first_paragraph: A durable travel cloak with weatherproof shoulders that sits comfortably over bedrolls and quivers.Named for the orange road banners of the southern courier guild.

heading: Sunvault Defender Plate
first_paragraph: A ceremonial breastplate with gold-lined channels that refract divine light into a halo at dawn.Carried out of a sealed sun-vault after a century without daylight.

heading: Verdant Duelist Saber
first_paragraph: A slim saber-longsword hybrid with a lively tip and vine-work guard built for quick woodland skirmishes.Favored by forest wardens who move lightly through fern paths and cedar mist.
